Petition For Instructions Re Payment Of Trustee Fees In Related Action
Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-04-286107 Estate of Anne Shirley Outin and
PTR-18-302220 The Anne S. Outin Revocable Living Trust.

Appearances: William Holsinger appeared by zoom for and with Debra Dolch (trustee/administrator) by zoom.
Courtney Sorenson appeared by zoom for Royal National Institute for the Blind (ultimate beneficiary).
Genevieve Duran appeared by zoom for Carr McClellan (former firm for the executor/trustee).

The court asked what the path forward is to ensure that Mrs. Dolch gets paid for her services performed and stated that its thought is to tell the appropriate party to seek compensation in the estate matter characterizing her work on the trust as extraordinary fees. Mr. Holsinger stated that because the trust came out of the estate, he believes that the court could find that Thorpe v Reed does not apply, and that fees could be paid out of the trust action, but he has no objection to the court's suggestion. Mrs. Sorenson and Mrs. Duran have no objections. Mr. Holsinger stated that the petitions for final distribution are in process and will be done fairly shortly. The Petition for Instructions Re Payment of Trustee Fees in Related Action, and the Petition for Instructions Re Payment of Trustee Fees are under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belem. Not reported.

Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Continued To Apr-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicole Warmerdam appeared by telephone for Debra Dolch (Petitioner).

The Court noted that there is no original partial inventory and appraisal ("I&A"), that a corrected I&A was filed and expressed concern about how the date of death values managed to go up, but bond was not increased. Ms. Warmerdam stated that they filed a partial I&A on January 23rd so that the assets could be valued by the Administrator, that they then needed a Referee to appraise the assets so they filed a final corrected I&A on February 15th, that the February 28th I&A was filed in error and was refiled in the trust, and that she will file something to correct this. With regard to the accounting, Ms. Warmerdam stated that Ms. Dolch is attempting to obtain bank statements, that they are having trouble with some of the financial institutions, that Bank of America requested a death certificate for the prior Administrator, that they would not accept an informational death certificate, that Ms. Dolch contacted the Daughter of the prior Administrator and that the Daughter has a death certificate and some bank records that may be helpful. In addition, Ms. Warmerdam stated that she could probably have the accounting done in about three months out, that the assets are at $420,065.30 and that they have an ex parte petition to increase bond ready to be filed. Status on filing of the accountings is continued to April 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the corrected inventory and appraisal is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Ms. Warmerdam stated that Ms. Dolch intends to proceed with an accounting from her date of appointment forward, that she has not discussed the issue of prior accountings with Ms. Dolch, and that she has bifurcated the assets and that she will file an amended inventory.
